one_line_takeaway: MEMSAD is a write-time, calibration-based cosine-similarity anomaly detector for RAG-agent memory poisoning with a proven gradient-coupling guarantee against continuous evasion, but its own experiments show it is evaded 80-100% of the time by simple discrete synonym substitution, and only a composite multi-defense ensemble (not MEMSAD alone) reaches TPR=1.00/FPR=0.00 across all three tested attacks.
key_finding: Reproducing AgentPoison under the triggered-query evaluation protocol specified in the original paper's Algorithm 2 (rather than the plain-query protocol used in some prior reproductions) raises measured ASR-R from 0.25 to 1.00. Untriggered attacks are substantially diluted by corpus scale at |M|=1,000: MINJA ASR-R = 0.14 [0.13,0.15] and InjecMEM ASR-R = 0.07 [0.06,0.07], versus AgentPoison's 1.00. Measured ASR-A under production-aligned GPT-4o-mini (0.20) is 3.4x lower than the modelled ASR-A reported in Chen et al. 2024 (0.68); switching to a tool-use setting raises measured ASR-A to 0.48 [0.34, 0.62]. On the 3x5 attack-defense matrix, the Composite defense achieves TPR=1.00, FPR=0.00 against all three attacks; MEMSAD alone achieves TPR=0.40/AUROC=0.914 against MINJA and TPR=0.20/AUROC=0.816 against InjecMEM under plain calibration, but TPR=0.00 against plain (non-triggered) AgentPoison, rising to TPR=1.00, FPR=0.00, AUROC=1.000 with triggered calibration. Under an adaptive white-box adversary applying greedy synonym substitution (average 3.8-5.1 substitutions per entry), the paper reports 80-100% evasion of MEMSAD with retrieval-success degradation delta-ASR-R approximately 0, confirming the theoretically predicted synonym-invariance loophole (Proposition 13). MEMSAD+ (character n-gram extension) raises InjecMEM TPR from 0.00 (post-synonym-evasion) to 0.40 and the original (non-evaded) TPR from 0.20 to 0.60. In an Mem0 production validation, LLM-mediated storage reformulation drops AgentPoison ASR-R to 0.00, while raw vector stores remain fully exposed.
core_contribution: The paper (1) formalizes memory-poisoning attacks against retrieval-augmented LLM agents as a Stackelberg game spanning three access models (WRITE/QUERY/SINGLE), (2) introduces MEMSAD, a pre-ingestion (write-time) calibration-based anomaly detector whose score is a combination of max-cosine and mean-cosine similarity between a candidate memory entry and a rolling query history, (3) proves a 'gradient coupling theorem' showing that under an L2-normalized, differentiable encoder the max-cosine detection score's gradient is directionally identical to the retrieval-objective gradient, so continuous evasion of the detector necessarily degrades retrieval rank, (4) derives a certified per-entry detection radius, a minimax sample-complexity lower bound for threshold calibration (via Le Cam's method) that MEMSAD is shown to match up to log(1/delta) factors, and online-calibration regret bounds, (5) formally characterizes a 'synonym-invariance loophole' (discrete lexical substitution can evade the continuous-space guarantee) and proposes MEMSAD+, a character n-gram extension that partially closes it, and (6) reports a 3x5 attack-defense evaluation matrix (AgentPoison, MINJA, InjecMEM against Watermark, Validation, Proactive, MEMSAD, and a Composite ensemble) with bootstrap CIs, Bonferroni-corrected hypothesis tests, and Clopper-Pearson intervals. The paper also corrects what it identifies as a protocol inconsistency in prior AgentPoison evaluation (plain-query vs. triggered-query), reporting this raises measured ASR-R from 0.25 to 1.00 (a 4x increase) in their reproduction.
threat_model: Adversary S = (Qv, n, alpha) targets a set of victim queries Qv by injecting n poisoned passages P into the agent's persistent memory store M, under one of three escalating access models: WRITE (arbitrary token sequences, used by AgentPoison), QUERY (query-only, restricted to auto-storage outputs of legitimate queries, used by MINJA), and SINGLE (single static entry via retriever-agnostic anchors, used by InjecMEM). Success is measured via ASR-R (retrieval success: fraction of victim queries for which a poison is retrieved in top-k), ASR-A (probability the agent executes the adversarial action once poison is retrieved), and ASR-T = ASR-R x ASR-A. The interaction is framed as a Stackelberg game where the defender (MEMSAD or other filter) commits to a detection policy first and the adversary best-responds; the paper also analyzes an adaptive white-box adversary with full knowledge of the calibration statistics (mu-hat, sigma-hat, kappa) performing greedy synonym substitution to evade detection while preserving retrieval rank.
limitations: The paper itself states: Proposition 13 (the synonym-invariance loophole) marks a structural frontier that MEMSAD+ closes only partially, since paraphrase-trained encoders make synonym substitutions near-isometric in embedding space; the main evaluation corpus is 1,000 synthetic entries (the Natural Questions cross-corpus experiment only partially addresses this); the online-calibration regret bound assumes slow drift; the evasion-hardness reduction (NP-hardness via CVP) is piecewise-linear; MEMSAD needs triggered calibration to detect AgentPoison effectively (plain calibration yields TPR=0.00 against it); watermarking, though achieving TPR=1.00 in simulation, fails silently when auto-storage bypasses the ingestion control point; and no single defense in the study dominates across all three access models, motivating the recommended composite/portfolio deployment. The Assumption 9 ('typical victim query') underlying the certified detection radius is stated to fail under a colluding user producing an outlier query history, at which point the deterministic certificate degrades to a probabilistic one.

datasets_used: Synthetic 1,000-entry benign memory corpus across 7 categories (task reminders, calendar events, user preferences, factual knowledge, document references, configuration settings, conversation history)
Natural Questions (Kwiatkowski et al., 2019) knowledge passages combined with synthetic filler to |M|=1,000, for cross-corpus generalization
quantitative_results: AgentPoison triggered-protocol reproduction: ASR-R rises from 0.25 (plain-query) to 1.00 (triggered-query), a 4x increase

Table 2 attack results at |M|=1,000: AgentPoison ASR-R=1.00, ASR-A(GPT-2)=0.90, ASR-A(4o-mini)=0.20, Benign Acc=1.00; MINJA ASR-R=0.14 [0.13,0.15], ASR-A(GPT-2)=0.17, ASR-A(4o-mini)=0.00, Benign Acc=1.00; InjecMEM ASR-R=0.07 [0.06,0.07], ASR-A(GPT-2)=0.00, ASR-A(4o-mini)=0.00, Benign Acc=0.98

Table 3 defense matrix (kappa=2.0 for MEMSAD): Watermark TPR=1.00/1.00/1.00, FPR=0.00/0.00/0.00 (AP/MJ/IM), AUROC avg 0.991; Validation TPR=0.60/0.40/0.80; Proactive TPR=1.00/0.07/0.60; MEMSAD (combined) TPR=0.00(plain, 1.00 triggered)/0.40/0.20, AUROC avg 0.867; Composite TPR=1.00/1.00/1.00, FPR=0.00/0.00/0.00
MEMSAD AUROC at kappa=2.0: 0.914 (MINJA), 0.816 (InjecMEM), 0.870 (AgentPoison plain), 1.000 (AgentPoison, triggered calibration)

Table 4 adaptive-adversary synonym-evasion results: AgentPoison evasion=1.00, delta-ASR-R=0.00, 4.2 substitutions/entry, similarity delta=-0.01; MINJA evasion=0.80, delta-ASR-R=0.00, 3.8 subs/entry, delta=-0.02; InjecMEM evasion=1.00, delta-ASR-R=0.00, 5.1 subs/entry, delta=-0.01
MEMSAD+ raises InjecMEM TPR from 0.00 (post-synonym-evasion) to 0.40, and original (non-evaded) TPR from 0.20 to 0.60
Calibration bound validation: theory predicts |tau_N - tau*| <= 0.022 at N=200; observed 0.014
Compound exposure: MINJA at ASR-R=0.14 reaches 90% cumulative compromise within 4 sessions
Cross-corpus (Natural Questions): AgentPoison ASR-R=1.00 +/- 0.00; MINJA ASR-R rises to 0.58 +/- 0.06 under topical homogeneity
Mem0 production validation: LLM-mediated storage reformulation drops AgentPoison ASR-R to 0.00; raw vector stores remain fully exposed
baselines_compared: Watermark (unigram watermarking scheme at write time, z-threshold 1.5)
Proactive (mean cosine similarity against 16 domain probes, threshold 0.19)
Composite (weighted ensemble of Watermark/Validation/Proactive with weights 0.50/0.20/0.30)
Energy Score OOD baseline (AUROC 0.945 vs. MEMSAD's 1.000 on triggered AgentPoison)
GPT-4o-mini text-level sanitization (described as complementary but 1000x slower than MEMSAD)
